scope.summary: Add the local-only doctor OAuth diagnostic, including verified expiry time, without changing opaque HTTP authentication responses.
out_of_scope: Live OAuth issuer or Oracle connectivity.

entry_points.name: Local oraclemcp doctor oauth --token CLI diagnostic
entry_points.trace: cargo test -p oraclemcp doctor_oauth -- --nocapture passed the command parser and local diagnostic tests. The diagnostic uses the resolved local OAuth verifier without starting an HTTP listener; its tested output distinguishes malformed, bad signature, verified expiry epoch, missing claim, unsupported algorithm, type, nbf, issuer, audience, and scope refusals while redacting token material.

entry_points.name: HTTP OAuth rejection challenge
entry_points.trace: target/sageraven-b2c/debug/deps/c1_oauth_literal_jwt-c1adf14f58878d22 c1_negative_tokens_share_one_opaque_challenge --nocapture passed after the scoped core test build. The TokenError::Expired match remains token-free audit classification only; rejected bearer responses retain the uniform invalid_token challenge.

limitations: Scoped tests only; the repository-wide Required gate was not run because main was already red for unrelated golden snapshots.
No live OAuth issuer, HTTP listener, or Oracle instance was exercised.
